name: Cross-Account IAM Role Assumption with MFA
description: Securely assume IAM roles across AWS accounts using MFA-protected temporary credentials for least-privilege access.
trendingReason: Cross-account access patterns are central to AWS Organizations and multi-account security strategies adopted by most teams.
prerequisites: AWS CLI v2 installed and configured
IAM role with trust policy allowing your account
MFA device registered on your IAM user

steps.title: Get your MFA device ARN
steps.description: List the virtual MFA devices associated with your IAM user to retrieve the ARN needed for authentication.
steps.tip: You can also find your MFA ARN in the IAM console under your user's Security credentials tab.

steps.title: Get a session token with MFA
steps.description: Request temporary session credentials by providing your MFA device ARN and a current TOTP code from your authenticator app.
steps.warning: The TOTP code expires every 30 seconds. Enter the command quickly after generating a fresh code.

steps.title: Assume the cross-account role
steps.description: Use the MFA-authenticated session to assume a role in the target account. This returns a new set of temporary credentials scoped to that role.
steps.tip: Add --duration-seconds to extend the session up to the role's MaxSessionDuration (default 1 hour, max 12 hours).

steps.title: Export the temporary credentials
steps.description: Set the returned temporary credentials as environment variables so subsequent AWS CLI commands use the assumed role.
steps.tip: Use jq to parse the JSON output cleanly. Install it with your package manager if not available.

steps.title: Verify the assumed identity
steps.description: Confirm that your CLI session is now operating under the assumed role in the target account.
steps.tip: The output should show the target account ID and the assumed role ARN, not your original user.

steps.title: Unset credentials when done
steps.description: Clear the temporary credentials from your environment to return to your default AWS profile.
steps.warning: Always unset assumed role credentials when finished to avoid accidentally running commands in the wrong account.

fullScript:
#!/bin/bash
# Cross-Account IAM Role Assumption with MFA
# Usage: ./assume-role.sh <target-role-arn> <mfa-arn> <mfa-code>

set -euo pipefail

TARGET_ROLE_ARN="${1:?Usage: $0 <target-role-arn> <mfa-arn> <mfa-code>}"
MFA_ARN="${2:?Provide your MFA device ARN}"
MFA_CODE="${3:?Provide your current MFA code}"
SESSION_NAME="cross-acct-$(date +%s)"

echo "Assuming role: $TARGET_ROLE_ARN"

CREDS=$(aws sts assume-role \
  --role-arn "$TARGET_ROLE_ARN" \
  --role-session-name "$SESSION_NAME" \
  --serial-number "$MFA_ARN" \
  --token-code "$MFA_CODE" \
  --duration-seconds 3600 \
  --output json)

export AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID=$(echo "$CREDS" | jq -r '.Credentials.AccessKeyId')
export AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY=$(echo "$CREDS" | jq -r '.Credentials.SecretAccessKey')
export AWS_SESSION_TOKEN=$(echo "$CREDS" | jq -r '.Credentials.SessionToken')

echo "Assumed role successfully:"
aws sts get-caller-identity

echo ""
echo "Credentials expire at: $(echo "$CREDS" | jq -r '.Credentials.Expiration')"
echo "Run 'unset AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY AWS_SESSION_TOKEN' to revert."

faq.question: How long do assumed role credentials last?
faq.answer: By default 1 hour. You can specify --duration-seconds up to the role's MaxSessionDuration setting, which can be configured up to 12 hours in the role's configuration.

faq.question: Can I use named profiles instead of exporting environment variables?
faq.answer: Yes. Add a profile in ~/.aws/config with role_arn, source_profile, and mfa_serial. The CLI will prompt for MFA and cache credentials automatically when you use --profile.

faq.question: What permissions does the trust policy need?
faq.answer: The target role's trust policy must include an sts:AssumeRole statement allowing your source account or specific IAM user/role ARN as the Principal, optionally with an MFA condition key (aws:MultiFactorAuthPresent).
